title: Safe Hormuz passage for Disha sparks hope for 34 India-bound ships
content: Better energy supplies may not bring immediate relief because extensive damage to facilities has created uncertainty over when normal operations will resume. India has a long-term gas supply contract with QatarEnergy’s Ras Laffan facility. UAE’s Habshan Gas Plant was also damaged, disrupting operations. Officials said 60% of the plant’s capacity has been restored. They expect recovery to reach 80% by the end of 2026 and full structural restoration in 2027.

title: PM Modi's trip a signal about Delhi’s reading of fractured Europe as well as Slovakia
content: Two-way trade, which first crossed $1 billion in 2024, reached $1.8 billion last year, with Indian exports of roughly $1.52 billion dwarfing imports of $284 million. That Modi chose this moment to visit Slovakia, becoming the first Indian prime minister to do so in 33 years, says as much about New Delhi’s reading of a fractured Europe as it does about Bratislava.

title: 150 kph and rising: Ashok Sharma gets India A call-up
content: Ashok Sharma's rapid rise continues as the 23-year-old pacer earns an India A call-up for the Sri Lanka tri-series after replacing injured Yudhvir Singh. Fresh from a domestic switch to Gujarat, the speedster now has a golden opportunity to push his case for higher honours.

title: ‘Air India flight AI 171 crash exposes payout void for victims on ground’
content: The absence of a dedicated statutory mechanism creates uncertainty over liability, quantum of compensation, timelines for relief, claims procedures and access to justice. It requested govt to establish a statutory ‘Ground Victims Compensation Framework’, introduce strict liability provisions so victims only need to establish the injury, death or property damage resulted from an aircraft accident, provide mandatory interim compensation within a prescribed period, prescribe minimum compensation standards, etc.

title: US senator says Tesla benefited from govt support, Elon Musk replies
content: Elon Musk has refuted claims that his companies, Tesla and SpaceX, owe their success to government aid, stating such incentives represent less than 2% of their value. The debate ignited between Senators Mike Lee and Ed Markey over taxpayer contributions to Musk's wealth. Musk asserted that removing EV tax credits actually boosted Tesla's market share.

title: Bangladesh PM’s adviser made to wait 2 hrs for ‘verification’, exits India
content: Dhaka Monday summoned Indian charge d’affaires Pawan Badhe to convey “strong displeasure” over the alleged treatment meted out to Zahed, a policy and strategy adviser to PM Tarique. Zahed had arrived in Delhi Sunday evening, on a regular Bangladeshi (green) passport with a SAARC visa, leading an official Bangladesh delegation for a meeting of the Indian Ocean Rim Association.

title: Ahead of retest, NEET glitches trouble students; two nabbed for promising paper
content: Meanwhile, Union home secretary Govind Mohan reviewed the preparedness of state govts for the NEET(UG) retest with a special focus on student convenience, security arrangements, and the integrity of the process. He directed states and central agencies to ensure that security protocols are strictly followed. NTA said it is working towards resolving all issues, including refunds. Till Monday morning, over four lakh aspirants had downloaded their admit cards.

title: Almost a year after giving engineers Claude and Cursor, Disney says, 'minimise AI-coded products'
content: Disney is urging its engineers to use AI tools like Claude and Cursor efficiently, focusing on boosting development speed without compromising code quality. The company aims to prevent AI-generated products from failing post-release, emphasizing productivity over excessive AI token usage. This strategic push follows a failed billion-dollar partnership with OpenAI.

title: Fifth unit of Kudankulam nuclear power project likely to be operational by year-end
content: NPCIL said, "This milestone reflects the close coordination and dedicated efforts of NPCIL and Atomstroyexport, Russian Federation. The successful erection of the vessel demonstrates the steady progress being made towards the completion of Unit 5, reinforcing NPCIL's commitment towards nation’s clean energy transition," it said. The Kudankulam site comprises six units, each of 1,000 MW capacity.

title: NCERT to restore original ‘Dancing Girl’ of Mohenjo-daro in class IX textbooks
content: The image appears in the opening chapter, “History of Arts”, of Madhurima, NCERT’s new class IX arts education textbook. In the version carried in the textbook, the torso of the figurine appears visually altered compared to photographs of the original artefact, with shading used across the upper body that obscures anatomical details visible in the sculpture.

title: Tanker ignored 60 warnings: US on strike that killed 3 Indians
content: The latest remarks by a US official sequencing developments that led to the attack seemed aimed at reassuring Indian authorities that US forces had not acted in a reckless manner in launching strikes on Settebello and two other foreign-flagged ships in the past week. However, the US is yet to express any regret for the deaths of Indian mariners.

title: Billionaire Mark Cuban warns 'Elons of the world' may see their wealth wiped out
content: Billionaire Mark Cuban warns even the wealthiest entrepreneurs face potential fortune loss from stock market crashes. He highlighted his past wealth exceeding Musk and Bezos combined, emphasizing his focus on passion over rankings. Cuban also voiced concerns about wealth concentration and shared his innovative strategy for safeguarding his $1.

title: Touchdown, salute, history: NCR’s 2nd international airport goes live
content: As someone chasing this mirage since 2006, I excitedly woke up at 3am in the city of nawabs to get ready for the flight. News of the US-Iran peace deal promised to remove the dark clouds over aviation and seemed like a good portent for the new airport. A contingent of around 80 business executives also boarded the flight, raising visions of the now sleepy Agra expressway resembling the Dwarka or Gurgaon expressways in the coming years with both sides of the stretch getting developed.

title: James Harden's arrest draws attention as mug shot emerges
content: James Harden’s mug shot was released after his arrest during a traffic stop in Houston early Saturday morning. Houston Police said a handgun was found on the seat of his vehicle and was not secured in a holster. The Cavaliers guard was charged with misdemeanor unlawful carrying of a weapon, released on a $100 bond, and is due in court later this month.

title: Did Joe Alwyn find new love before Taylor Swift’s wedding to Kelce
content: Taylor Swift and her future husband, the Kansas City Chiefs’ star player and popular tight end, Travis Kelce are just days away from tying the knot but it seems her ex boyfriend, Joe Alwyn has dominated headlines since the last few days as it has sparked chaos among fans. While the popular actor Joe Alwyn likes to keep things private in his life, a recent rumor about him romancing pop star Ariana Grande has sparked chaos.

title: NFL trade rumor: Raiders and Commanders emerge top fits for Patriots Star
content: The New England Patriots’ star player, Kayshon Boutte, has been actively looking to be traded from the team for the last few weeks. as many fans remain concerned about his future in the league as he seeks a trade. Amid all the mess, the Las Vegas Raiders and the Washington Commanders have emerged as potential landing spots for the star player.

title: Brent slips on US-Iran deal, situation may ease for India
content: Brent crude slipped to nearly $83 per barrel Monday, a significant development for India, which imports nearly 88% of its crude oil requirement. The rate at which Indian refiners procure crude, was recorded at $86.77 per barrel Friday, the latest available price, and is expected to decline following the fall in global energy prices.

title: UK follows in Australia’s footsteps, announces ban on social media for under-16s
content: AI “romantic companion” chatbots will have to enforce a minimum age of 18 and under-16s will be banned from live-streaming themselves and from making contact with strangers, including on gaming platforms. YouTube was among the first companies to criticise the ban. A spokesperson said: “YouTube is a vital resource for young people, educators and parents. Blanket bans push kids out of such curated, supervised, beneficial experiences, and towards anonymous, less safe services.”

title: India & Slovakia upgrade ties, seek reform of global bodies
content: After a bilateral meeting between Modi and his counterpart Robert Fico, the two sides announced over a dozen outcomes, including a joint working group on counterterrorism, a letter of intent for promoting defence cooperation and an MoU on labour migration. Modi is on a two-day visit to Bratislava and will return to France on Tuesday for the G7 summit.

title: Diesel price hike: Govt to compensate National Highways builders
content: Earlier, road transport ministry rolled out the scheme for additional payment for increase in bitumen prices by updating it every month. Prices of bitumen used in highway projects have increased from Rs 49,000 per tonne on Feb 28 to Rs 80,000 a tonne on June 1. “The new policy will be out soon. Highway agencies will pay the differential amount between the base rate and prevailing price of bulk diesel,” said an official.

title: PM Modi welcomes US-Iran deal, hopes for freedom of navigation soon
content: India had in April too welcomed a temporary ceasefire announced by both sides, notwithstanding the fact that it had been brokered with help from Pakistan. On this occasion, Pakistan said after the announcement by both Trump and Iran that it would host in Geneva this week the signing of the agreement, which is expected to formally mark the end of the conflict that started on Feb 28.

title: Meeting PM hasn’t changed Jantar Mantar protest plan for statehood: J&K CM
content: On June 3, Omar had called a meeting of his party’s MLAs and MPs at Dachigam national park on Srinagar’s outskirts for a day-long strategy session that ended with a decision to protest in New Delhi on the opening day of Parliament’s monsoon session demanding restoration of J&K’s statehood. J&K lost its statehood on Aug 5, 2019, when the Centre abrogated Article 370, revoked the region's special constitutional status and bifurcated the former state into two Union territories, J&K and Ladakh.

title: Govt hikes export duty on diesel to Rs 14/litre, ATF to Rs 12.5/litre
content: Under the revised rates, the special additional excise duty (SAED) on diesel exports has been raised to Rs 14 per litre from Rs 13.5 per litre. The duty on ATF exports has been increased to Rs 12.5 per litre from Rs 9.5 per litre. The export duty on petrol remains unchanged at Rs 1.5 per litre. According to a notification issued by the finance ministry, the revised rates will come into effect from June 16.

title: SC notice to Centre, Punjab over failure to implement RTE
content: Though RTE and RTI were among the main planks on which AAP acquired political salience at national level, the PIL by Jagmohan Singh Raju said RTE Act’s mandate for a quota of 25% for children belonging to weaker and disadvantaged sections of society in schools remained largely unimplemented in state.

title: No govt, no OTS deal can curtail citizen’s right to move court: Supreme Court
content: Nearly four decades ago, Haryana govt had acquired more than 1,500 acres of land at Panchkula for Sectors 24 to 28 and sold the plots to individuals. Later, various authorities one after the other increased the compensation payable to farmers for their land over the next decade. Intimating plot buyers earlier, Haryana Shahari Vikas Pradhikaran (HSVP) sent additional demand notices to them for a corresponding increase in plot prices.

title: Protecting Amarnath yatris collective duty of Kashmiris, says Mehbooba
content: “Every Amarnath pilgrim who visits us is a guest of Kashmir. They carry the story of our land, our people and our values to every corner of India. It is our responsibility to ensure they leave with memories of our love, warmth and hospitality,” Mehbooba said, addressing her party workers in Pahalgam.

title: 78% older persons experienced at least one climate-related hazard in past 3 years
content: While most affected by heatwaves stay indoors (90%) and increase water intake (81%), illness still rises (74%), existing conditions worsen (44%), and healthcare access becomes difficult (33%). Released on Monday to mark World Elder Abuse Awareness Day observed each year on June 15, the study calls for focus on "Climate Resilient Ageing: Ensuring Care, Dignity and Agency".

title: Horoscope today: Astrological predictions for June 16, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for June 16, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: UPSC Prelims 2026 results out: 13,343 candidates qualify for Mains exam
content: The UPSC has announced the Civil Services Preliminary Examination 2026 results, with 13,343 candidates advancing to the Main Examination. These qualifiers must complete online formalities, including fee payment and cadre preference submission, by June 28 to remain eligible for recruitment into services like IAS, IFS, and IPS.

title: Ron MacLean apologizes after Stanley Cup final comments
content: Veteran broadcaster Ron MacLean was at the center of a controversy during Game 6 of the NHL Stanley Cup Final between the Edmonton Oilers and Florida Panthers. The comments from the long-time Sportsnet personality on the broadcast drew criticism from viewers, and he later issued a public apology during the game. The incident was quickly picked up by the latest NHL news roundup and NHL updates.

title: Jaipur woman kills 5-year-old over suspected affair involving her husband
content: A five-year-old girl was allegedly murdered by her neighbour in Jaipur's Harmada area. The accused, Gudiya Devi, reportedly harboured suspicion of an illicit relationship between the victim's mother and her husband, and also held a grudge over a water-fetching quarrel. The child's body was found concealed in a divan at the accused's residence.

title: 12 cases of sexual assault on children, women in 24 hours shock Chennai
content: Residents also expressed concerns over the safety of children living in worker settlements, saying that many parents leave for work early in the morning and return only in the evening. They urged policemen to strengthen security measures in the area.

title: 'Orchestrated by BJP': CJP's big claim after attack on Abhijeet Dipke; 5 arrested
content: Abhijeet Dipke, founder of the Cockroach Janta Party, was slapped by youths during a protest in Jaipur.  The incident occurred at Shaheed Smarak during a demonstration over alleged NEET paper leaks and unemployment.  Police arrested five individuals following a scuffle between the accused and Dipke's supporters.  The protest continued with participants demanding action against corruption and examination irregularities.

title: 'Forced to chant in Urdu, eat meat': Army officer's wife claims rape, forced conversion in Nagpur
content: A married woman in Nagpur has accused her former acquaintance and his associates of rape, blackmail, and forced religious conversion. She alleges she was drugged, photographed in compromising positions, and extorted. The accused reportedly performed conversion rituals, declared her married to one of them, and forced her to eat meat before further assaults.

title: ‘Let the oil flow’: What Trump’s peace deal with Iran, Strait of Hormuz opening means for India
content: The Strait of Hormuz closure has been responsible for global crude oil prices surging and resulting in an economic shock for the world. India, dependent on imports for almost 90% of its needs, has seen the impact.

title: Watch: Egg hurled at TMC MLA Kunal Ghosh outside Mamata Banerjee's residence
content: TMC MLA Kunal Ghosh faced an egg attack outside Mamata Banerjee's Kalighat residence. A youth threw an egg at Ghosh as he spoke to reporters. The accused claimed Ghosh had committed atrocities. Ghosh condemned the incident as planned hooliganism and questioned security. He stated the act would not deter him. Police action was pending.

title: The Mamdani Effect? Arsenal, New York Knicks title wins create a new sporting superstition
content: While the Mamdani Effect might sound like a boost for progressive causes since the election of New York’s first Muslim mayor, it’s actually what The Athletic called a Zohran Mamdani sports summer, after two of his teams, Arsenal and Knicks, ended historic trophy droughts.

title: Sooryavanshi, Tilak, controversy and Super Over drama: How it unfolded
content: A Tri-Nation A Series match between India A and Sri Lanka A descended into chaos with a controversial tie, a Super Over, and heated exchanges. Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was at the center of the drama, involved in arguments with umpires and Sri Lankan players after India's defeat.

title: Heatwaves and ozone together increase India’s cardiac deaths: study
content: A peer-reviewed study reports that surface ozone reaches 85-110 μg/m³ in northern India during heatwaves and exceeds the WHO guideline of 70 μg/m³ in every region of the country. In 2024, roughly 830 more deaths were reported during the heatwave than the preceding days.

title: Social media, video platforms surpass traditional outlets as leading news sources: report
content: The report is based on an online survey of nearly 1 lakh people across 48 markets worldwide. The study states that consumption patterns have changed with audiences’ growing disengagement with news

title: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke manhandled, slapped during protest in Jaipur; two youth detained
content: Police detain two youths in connection with the incident; Dipke’s supporters catch hold of the accused and assault them before police intervened

title: Merger with NCPI may allow Trinamool rebels to vote in Lok Sabha prior to any ruling on disqualification
content: Since the Centre may bring the Delimitation Bill as early as the monsoon session of Parliament, it becomes crucial that this bloc of rebel Trinamool MPs, which has promised support to the NDA, gets to vote when the Bills are taken up

title: After criticism, NCERT to restore original 'Dancing Girl' image in school textbook
content: Iconic bronze figurine of Mohenjo-daro has its bare torso covered in the latest NCERT arts textbook for Class 9; unlike the altered image, the Dancing Girl in a Class 6 textbook appears in a form closer to the original bronze sculpture

title: Kakoli seeks Kalyan’s expulsion from Lok Sabha, writes to Speaker
content: Rebel Trinamool Congress MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar wrote to Speaker Om Birla and cited repeated verbal abuse and misogynistic conduct during House proceedings allegedly by Kalyan Banerjee

title: How Tamil Nadu severed religion and caste from politics
content: Tamil movies and Tamil Nadu politics are an inseparable jodi that has largely been of benefit to the State socially and economically

title: Cheers and selfies mark launch of women’s free travel scheme in Ernakualm
content: Passengers celebrate the district-level launch aboard an Ordinary KSRTC bus with sweets, selfies and zero-fare tickets

title: NEET security measures reviewed, special focus on exam integrity
content: CRPF and CISF personnel with a clean service record have been deployed to provide security to the exam papers as they are taken to examination centres.

title: SIT starts probe into Ram Mandir donations issue, reaches Ram temple complex
content: The State government constituted the three-member SIT at the request of the Shree Ram Teerth Kshetra Trust

title: Odisha marine fishers stop going to sea for fishing due to “abnormal” diesel cost
content: Odisha Marine Fish Producers Association (OMFPA) said many boats remain idle at the harbour because operators can no longer afford the fuel costs

title: We are the largest parliamentary force from West Bengal: NCPI in a social media post
content: 20 rebel MPs of Trinamool Congress have announced their decision to merge with NCPI after they met  the Speaker of Lok Sabha Om Birla in Delhi on Sunday and submitted a letter to that effect

title: Sikh Clergy declares Punjab CM Mann ‘betrayer of Guru’; AAP dismisses forensic reports
content: A position of Chief Minister is respectable; but Chief Minister Bhagwant Singh (Mann) lied at the Akal Takht (about the video), says Akal Takht Jathedar Giani Kuldeep Singh Gargajj

title: Bombay High Court directs State to secure former Judge’s safety
content: The Bench said both Justice Patel, his wife and also daughter, who are in Mumbai, should be adequately protected

title: Boy dies of shigellosis; two cases reported in Kannur
content: The Malappuram district administration has announced a two-week campaign against communicable diseases from June 17

title: Warmth of hospitality during Amarnath yatra will defeat hate poltics: Mehbooba Mufti
content: She urged a peaceful people’s initiative to ensure every Yatri feels welcomed, respected, and protected

title: Priyank Kharge’s call for transparency in RSS is ‘driven by politics’: Mohan Bhagwat
content: RSS does not receive government funds and hence needs no registration, Bhagwat says. Kharge wrote to the RSS chief on June 13, seeking details about the organisation’s registration status, sources of funding, income, expenditure, and assets.

title: Don’t allow hydrocarbon projects in Cuddalore, says Thangam Thennarasu
content: Mr. Thennarasu said the State government should oppose the projects in Parankipettai as they would affect natural resources and the livelihoods of fishermen

title: ED attaches ₹13.83-crore assets in Uttarakhand SC/ST scholarship scam case
content: The ED alleges that educational institutions fraudulently obtained scholarship funds released by the Social Welfare Department of the Uttarakhand government by showing ineligible, non-genuine, and non-verifiable students as beneficiaries.

title: U.S. model of consumption will require six Earths if adopted by India, says RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat
content: Bhagwat says economic prosperity must be guided by restraint, ethical conduct and concern for the welfare of others

title: Indigenous soil, water management sustaining Nagaland terrace farming, says study
content: The researchers say the findings could help integrate development policies and projects with culturally sensitive agricultural methods; the study goes deeper into the social, cultural, and ecological system that supports the practice

title: Bangladesh PM’s adviser returns from Delhi airport; says he was ‘humiliated’ by immigration
content: Rahman was held for more than two hours before he was cleared to enter Delhi; by that time, he had made up his mind to return

title: ED questions Abhishek for 11 hours in primary school jobs scam case
content: Officials said Trinamool Congress general secretary Abhishek Banerjee was questioned over the alleged money trail in the scam.

title: Displaced Kashmiri Pandits seek structured engagement, genocide recognition, and property recovery
content: A week-long heritage tour and a two-day international conference in Srinagar voiced a deep yearning for a return to their homeland

title: Monsoon tracker highlights: Heatwave alert for seven districts, thunderstorm forecast in 10 districts for tomorrow in Telangana
content: The monsoon activity in Andhra Pradesh has remained subdued thus far, with 17 out of the 28 districts reporting deficient rainfall between June 1 and June 14

title: VCK hopes to address depoliticisation of youth through training programme
content: The first session in the series, named  ‘Present Times’, was addressed on Monday (June 15, 2026) by party president Thol. Thirumavalavan and general secretary D. Ravikumar

title: Karnataka govt. asks RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat to send representatives to explain legal status on registration of organisation
content: Quoting the 2025-2026 report of the Akhil Bharatiya Pratinidhi Sabha, the highest decision-making body of the RSS, Home Minister Priyank Kharge has said that the organisation has significant footprint in Karnataka, with 4,127 daily shakhas, 1,389 weekly milans, and 60 monthly mandalis

title: Will remind Centre of Statehood promise at Jantar Mantar: Omar Abdullah
content: Says National Conference government will continue pursuing Statehood “through all democratic and constitutional means”.

title: ED files prosecution complaint in $20 million crypto fraud case
content: The investigation has revealed the accused and his associates allegedly created and used fake websites resembling the genuine Coinbase platform to deceive victims, and obtain their login credentials and other details

title: SAAP Chairman praises para swimmers for shining at national tourney
content: Andhra Pradesh won 29 medals, including 15 gold, eight silver, and six bronze in the 26th Junior and Sub-Junior National Para Swimming Championship held recently in Bengaluru

title: Three months on, family of custodial torture victim refuses to receive his body
content: The Madurai Bench of the Madras High Court directed Akash Delison’s father to receive the body by 5 p.m. on Monday (June 15, 2026), adding that otherwise, the State would be directed to dispose of it

title: Dr. YSR Horticultural University launches drone technology training for students
content: Addressing students and faculty members, university Vice-Chancellor says Unmanned Aerial Vehicles are increasingly being used for crop surveillance, precision farming, land mapping, research activities and efficient farm management

title: Flight-test of indigenous, long-range land attack cruise missile successful
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh congratulates DRDO team and industry partners on the success, describes it as a major achievement for India’s defence research ecosystem

title: Protestors seek return of pledged gold in Bank of Baroda ‘loan scam’
content: Raising slogans such as “Return our gold,” the affected farmers, small traders, artisans, and other account holders accuse bank officials of misappropriating gold pledged against loans during a protest at the Eluru Collectorate

title: Ahmedabad Cyber Crime Police bust two networks running NEET scams
content: In two separate cases, they uncovered a Telegram scam falsely promising leaked NEET-UG re-exam papers while a 19-year-old from Bihar was arrested for allegedly hacking hundreds of student accounts to steal their NEET refunds

title: Minister distributes subsidised tractors, equipment to farmers in Palnadu
content: Participating in the coalition government’s second anniversary celebrations, Gottipati Ravi Kumar says the government has fulfilled all promises made while in the opposition and is committed to balancing welfare initiatives with economic development

title: Cultural events, medical check-ups mark ‘Yatri Suvidha Diwas’ at Vijayawada airport
content: Addressing the media, Airport Director says the programmes have been organised to seek feedback from passengers and work on improving passenger-centric facilities

title: CID to probe the death of Bengali actor Rahul Arunodoy Bandyopadhyay
content: The 42-year-old actor, died while shooting for the television series Bhole Baba Par Karega at Talsari beach on March 29

title: Disqualification proceedings against four former MLAs will be taken to their logical end, A-G assures Madras HC
content: AIADMK whip urges the court to prevent byelections in four constituencies vacated by the MLAs and another litigant insists upon a CBI probe into circumstances that led to their resignations

title: In Manipur, mob protests hospital treatment of 3 Kukis injured in armed attack
content: Kuki organisations accuse Naga extremist groups of attacking a village in Kangpokpi district with bombs

title: Centre Hikes Windfall Tax On Diesel
content: The government on Monday hiked windfall gains tax on exports of diesel and aviation turbine fuel (ATF), while retaining the levy on petrol for the fortnight beginning June 16.

title: Russian Drone Hits Zoo In Ukraine's Kharkiv, Kills Animals
content: A Russian drone struck enclosures at the zoo in Ukraine's second-largest city of Kharkiv on Monday, killing rabbits and inflicting stress on other animals, prosecutors said.

title: B-52 Bomber Crashes After Takeoff At US Military Base In California
content: A B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff Monday morning at a U.S. Air Force base in California's Mojave Desert, officials said.

title: Netanyahu Says Will Run In Upcoming Israeli Elections
content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday said he intended to run in elections scheduled for later this year, as he faced domestic criticism over his handling of the Middle East war and its aftermath.

title: Rain Alert Highlights: Delhi Records Below-Normal Maximum Temperature, Rain Predicted For Today
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Delhi recorded a max temperature of 38.5C with light rainfall and thunderstorms forecast today.

title: Over 1,000 Civilians Died In Sudan Drone Strikes In First 5 Months Of 2026
content: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Turk said his office has documented a "sharp increase" in drone attacks as well as rape and sexual violence in the Sudan war, now in its fourth year.

title: Option Trading, Greenshoe: What's Next For SpaceX Stock After Hit IPO
content: The IPO includes a so-called greenshoe option, a standard feature of most large US stock market listings that acts like a safety valve that keeps the stock price from going crazy one way or another in its first month.

title: Abhishek Banerjee Questioned For Over 10 Hours By Probe Agency ED
content: After coming out of the ED office at around 10.15 pm, he alleged that summonses like this are attempts to destroy the opposition forces.

title: No Livestreaming, Curfews: How UK's Social Media Ban For Under-16s Will Work
content: The government said regulator Ofcom would conduct a rapid study to establish the best way to verify if someone is over 16, and would have a new enforcement strategy and funding.

title: Microsoft Sued By Shareholders Over Defrauding Them, Inflating Stock Price
content: About $357 billion of market value was erased, and Microsoft's stock suffered its biggest one-day decline in nearly six years.

title: 217 Demolitions, 237 Properties Sealed: Delhi Crackdown On Illegal Buildings
content: The civic body has also issued 330 show-cause notices for unauthorised construction, 151 sealing show-cause notices, and passed 91 demolition orders as part of the ongoing crackdown.

title: "Ships Starting To Move": Trump Claims Oil Tankers Crossing Strait Of Hormuz
content: Pakistan announced on Sunday that the United States and Iran had agreed to an "immediate and permanent termination" of military operations, but the text of their peace deal has yet to be released.

title: Unpaid Debts, Undeclared Work? Sweden Can Now Legally Kick Out Immigrants
content: The law has been criticised by the opposition and human rights advocacy groups as arbitrary because decisions would be taken on behaviour that has not been deemed criminal.

title: 20 Years, 128 Witnesses: Ex-Congress Leader's Murder Trial Nears Verdict
content: The Special CBI Court was originally expected to pronounce its judgment in May 2026. However, the verdict was deferred and is now scheduled for June 16.

title: India, Slovakia Forge Comprehensive Partnership In PM Modi's First Visit
content: PM Modi's visit, hosted by Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico, produced a sweeping joint statement spanning defence, counter-terrorism, trade, technology, energy and culture

title: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: JD Vance To Lead Delegation For Iran Peace Deal Signing In Switzerland
content: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei reportedly denied that a memorandum of understanding would be signed.

title: 10 Years Of Marriage, 2 Children: Bangladeshi Woman Faces Deportation
content: Tarun Patel, a resident of Lambhvel village, met the woman on social media around a decade ago, and the two eventually entered into a relationship.

title: Once Leh's Only Drinking Water Source, Historic T-Trench Comes Back To Life
content: The spring-fed water body, which once supplied drinking water to the entire Leh city, had fallen into disuse amid unplanned construction. Its restoration has boosted capacity from 3 lakh to 5 lakh litres a day while helping recharge groundwater.

title: BJP says Gehlot's remarks against Hindutva reflect Emergency-era mindset
content: BJP lashed out at former CM Ashok Gehlot for stating that he would have banned the governing party for its religious politics . Gehlot had said that his remarks are more a reflection on the authoritarian impulses of Congress than itself . he said that the constitution does not permit politics in the name of religion .

title: India extends two oldest water tribunals by 1 year
content: Vishwa Mohan is Senior Editor at The Times of India. He writes on environment, climate change, agriculture, water resources and clean energy, tracking policy issues and climate diplomacy. He has been covering Parliament since 2003 to see how politics shaped up domestic policy and India’s position at global platform. Before switching over to explore sustainable development issues, Vishwa had covered internal security and investigative agencies for more than a decade. Read More

title: Congress open to 'ghar wapsi' of ex-members
content:
Congress general secretary KC Venugopal (ANI photo)

NEW DELHI: Stoking the growing public chatter over merger of breakaway outfits with Congress, the AICC has expressed its readiness to accept its erstwhile members into the party fold. AICC general secretary (organisation) and Rahul Gandhi confidant K C Venugopal told TOI, “Everybody who believes in the Congress ideology can come back. There is no issue.” He said such mergers and homecomings will only strengthen the fight against “BJP-fascist forces”. “All the parties who want to join Congress and believe in the Congress ideology can approach us. We will welcome them,” he added. Venugopal, however, clarified that presently there are no such merger proposals under consideration of AICC. “We are hearing all these speculations from the media,” he claimed.

End of Article
